title: Melted Russian Cheese Dumplings
description: A cozy Russian dish featuring melted cheese, red pepper, and a whisk, perfect for a comforting meal, and suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, egg, red pepper, and salt until well combined.
Add the crumbly farmer's cheese to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the melted butter.
Place 4 of the dough circles in the skillet and cook for about 2 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough circles.
Serve the dumplings hot with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh dill, if desired.
